# discord-bot-onboarding
Description: Fully automates Discord bot registration in NeuroClaw from a minimal set of variables — token, name, default agent, and optional routing config.
Source: project
Triggers: set up a bot, add a new bot, register a bot, onboard a bot, new discord bot, create a bot for, add bot for agent
Tools: discord_register_bot, discord_add_channel_route, discord_set_auto_reply_guilds, discord_list_guilds, run_skill_script
Scripts: setup_bot.py
# Discord Bot Onboarding

Automate full Discord bot registration in NeuroClaw from a minimal set of variables.
The Discord Developer Portal step (creating the app and getting the token) is irreducibly manual —
everything after the token exists is handled by this skill.

## When to Activate
- User provides a Discord bot token and says "set it up"
- User says "add a new bot", "register a bot", "onboard a bot for [agent]"
- User hands a token and wants it assigned to a specific agent/channel

---

## Variables to Collect

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BOT_TOKEN` | ✅ | From Discord Developer Portal → Bot tab → Reset Token |
| `BOT_NAME` | ✅ | Display label for this bot config in NeuroClaw |
| `DEFAULT_AGENT` | ✅ | Agent name that handles all unrouted @mentions |
| `APPLICATION_ID` | Optional | Discord App ID — skips a startup REST call; find it on the General Info page |
| `GUILD_IDS` | Optional | Comma-separated server IDs where bot auto-replies without @mention |
| `CHANNEL_ROUTES` | Optional | JSON array: `[{"channel_id":"123","agent":"Jarvis","auto_reply":true}]` |

If the user doesn't provide APPLICATION_ID, it will be fetched automatically by the bot on connect.
If no GUILD_IDS or CHANNEL_ROUTES are provided, the bot registers with defaults and routes can be added later.

---

## Execution Procedure

### Step 1 — Validate & Plan
Run `setup_bot.py` with the collected variables.
The script validates the token format, parses channel routes, and generates OAuth2 invite URLs.
Review the output before proceeding.

```
run_skill_script(
  skill_name="discord-bot-onboarding",
  script="setup_bot.py",
  args=["--token", BOT_TOKEN, "--name", BOT_NAME, "--default-agent", DEFAULT_AGENT,
        "--app-id", APPLICATION_ID,          # omit if not provided
        "--guild-ids", GUILD_IDS,            # omit if not provided
        "--channel-routes", CHANNEL_ROUTES]  # omit if not provided
)
```

### Step 2 — Register the Bot
```
discord_register_bot(
  name=BOT_NAME,
  token=BOT_TOKEN,
  default_agent=DEFAULT_AGENT,
  application_id=APPLICATION_ID  # only if provided
)
```
→ Store the returned `bot_id` — needed for all subsequent calls.

### Step 3 — Add Channel Routes (if provided)
For EACH entry in CHANNEL_ROUTES:
```
discord_add_channel_route(
  bot_id=<from step 2>,
  channel_id=route.channel_id,
  agent=route.agent,
  auto_reply=route.auto_reply  # default false if not specified
)
```

### Step 4 — Set Auto-Reply Guilds (if provided)
```
discord_set_auto_reply_guilds(
  bot_id=<from step 2>,
  guild_ids=[GUILD_IDS split by comma]
)
```

### Step 5 — Deliver Results to User
Present:
- ✅ Bot registered (bot_id, name, default agent)
- ✅ Channel routes configured (list)
- ✅ Auto-reply guilds set (list)
- 🔗 OAuth2 invite URL — user must click this once per server
- ⚠️ Token is never echoed back; show first 10 + last 5 chars only

---

## OAuth2 Permissions Reference

| Use Case | Permissions Integer | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Standard (recommended) | `2147551232` | View channels, send messages, read history, slash commands |
| Administrator | `8` | Only for private/fully trusted servers |

Invite URL format:
`https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id={APP_ID}&permissions=2147551232&scope=bot%20applications.commands`

---

## Security Rules
- **Never echo the raw token** — preview only: first 10 chars + "..." + last 5 chars
- Prefer scoped permissions over Administrator for shared servers
- Confirm with user before adding auto-reply to any guild (it's aggressive behavior)
- If APPLICATION_ID is unknown, tell the user to find it on the Developer Portal General Info page

---

## What Remains Manual (Discord Policy — Cannot Automate)
1. Creating the Application in the Discord Developer Portal
2. Enabling the Bot and copying the token
3. Clicking the OAuth2 invite link to add the bot to a server (requires server Manage Server permission)

Everything else this skill handles automatically.
